req.title: List employees pagination
req.statement: The employee list shall be paginated at 50 per page with cursor-based pagination.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates cursor-based pagination at 50 records per page and a 422 error when limit exceeds 200. Test cases cover: (1) EP – a valid default page request returning up to 50 records with a cursor; (2) NEG – a limit of 201 triggering a 422; (3) BVA – the exact boundary limit of 200 returning 200 records successfully; (4) BVA – limit of 1 as the lower boundary; (5) STC – following the cursor from page 1 to page 2 to verify state continuity across pages.

tests.name: test_default_page_returns_50_employees
preconditions: User is authenticated and token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
At least 50 employee records exist in the target HR app
expected: Response body contains a 'data' array of exactly 50 items
Response body contains a non-null 'next_cursor' string
Each item in 'data' has an 'id' field
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_default_page_returns_50_employees(auth_token_fixture, base_url):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token_fixture}"}
    response = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees", headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert "data" in body, "Response must contain 'data' key"
    assert len(body["data"]) == 50, f"Expected 50 items, got {len(body['data'])}"
    assert body.get("next_cursor") is not None, "next_cursor must be present"
    for item in body["data"]:
        assert "id" in item, "Each employee record must have an 'id'"

tests.name: test_limit_above_200_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated and token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
expected: Response body contains an error or detail field indicating invalid limit

tests.name: test_limit_at_200_boundary_returns_200_ok
preconditions: User is authenticated and token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
At least 200 employee records exist in the target HR app
expected: Response body 'data' array contains at most 200 items
trace: R-WEB-107: 422 when limit > 200 (boundary at 200 must succeed)

tests.name: test_limit_at_1_lower_boundary_returns_single_record
preconditions: User is authenticated and token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
At least 1 employee record exists in the target HR app
actions: Assert HTTP 200 and exactly 1 record returned
expected: Response body 'data' array contains exactly 1 item
A 'next_cursor' field is present when more records exist
trace: R-WEB-107: paginated at 50 per page (lower boundary for limit param)

tests.name: test_cursor_pagination_advances_to_next_page
preconditions: User is authenticated and token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
At least 100 employee records exist so two full pages of 50 are available
expected: Page 1 returns HTTP 200 with 50 records and a non-null next_cursor
Page 2 returns HTTP 200 with up to 50 records
Page 2 records do not overlap with page 1 records (no duplicate IDs)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_cursor_pagination_advances_to_next_page(auth_token_fixture, base_url):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token_fixture}"}
    r1 = requests.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees", headers=headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 200
    body1 = r1.json()
    assert len(body1["data"]) == 50
    cursor = body1.get("next_cursor")
    assert cursor, "next_cursor must be present after page 1"
    r2 = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/api/employees",
        headers=headers,
        params={"cursor": cursor}
    )
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    body2 = r2.json()
    assert len(body2["data"]) > 0, "Page 2 must contain at least one record"
    ids_page1 = {item["id"] for item in body1["data"]}
    ids_page2 = {item["id"] for item in body2["data"]}
    assert ids_page1.isdisjoint(ids_page2), "Pages must not share employee IDs"
